shortDescription: US Embassy F-1 · SEVIS · I-20 from SEVP school required

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current MRV fee (USD $185) and SEVIS I-901 fee (USD $350 for F-1) on travel.state.gov and ice.gov before quoting.

alert.title: 214(b) ties at interview
alert.body: Most Indian refusals cite Section 214(b)—non-immigrant intent. Prepare client for concise, honest answers on ties, funds, and study plan.

about.label: Description
about.value: F-1 non-immigrant visa for full-time academic study at a US institution certified by SEVP. Requires valid I-20, SEVIS payment, DS-160, and consular interview.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Valid I-20 from SEVP school · Proof of funds · English readiness · Non-immigrant intent · Clean immigration history

about.label: Interview focus
about.value: Consular officer assesses genuine student intent, funds, and ties to India. Short, clear answers work better than memorised scripts.

about.label: Key authority
about.value: US Department of State (embassy/consulate) · SEVIS/ICE for I-901

about.label: After approval
about.value: F-1 visa foil in passport; enter US no earlier than 30 days before I-20 start. Maintain full-time enrollment and SEVIS compliance.

eligibility.note: Program and start date must be realistic
Covers tuition + living for at least first year

eligibility.criterion: No prior visa fraud or serious inadmissibility

redFlagsBanner: After 214(b) refusal, do not reapply with the same weak ties story. Strengthen funds evidence and study rationale before booking a new slot.

redFlags.title: Weak non-immigrant intent (214(b))
redFlags.description: Vague return plan, course mismatch, or answers suggesting permanent stay intent.
redFlags.fix: Course-specific study plan; tie program to India career; practice concise interview answers

redFlags.title: Insufficient or unseasoned funds
redFlags.description: Large recent deposits, sponsor income not matching funds, or weak loan documentation.
redFlags.fix: 4–6 month bank history; sponsor ITR + CA statement; education loan sanction letter

redFlags.title: I-20 / school credibility issues
redFlags.description: Unknown institution, course downgrade pattern, or start date passed.
redFlags.fix: Verify SEVP certification; align program with academic background

redFlags.title: DS-160 inconsistencies
redFlags.description: Dates, employment, or travel history mismatch with documents or prior applications.
redFlags.fix: Cross-check DS-160 against passport, I-20, and resume before submit

redFlags.title: Previous refusals not addressed
redFlags.description: Prior US or other country refusals hidden or unchanged circumstances.
redFlags.fix: Disclose honestly; explain what changed—stronger funds, clearer plan

redFlags.title: Over-rehearsed or argumentative interview
redFlags.description: Client memorises script or argues with officer.
redFlags.fix: Mock interview with natural answers; listen and respond briefly

faqs.a: No. Consular officers decide at interview. We prepare the file and coach for interview but never promise approval.
Typically tuition + living per I-20 for first year. Verify amount on I-20 financial page; include credible sponsor or loan evidence.
Immigration law presumes immigrant intent. F-1 applicants must convince officer they will return after studies—ties, funds, and credible study plan matter.
F-2 dependants may apply separately. F-2 cannot work. Assess funds for entire family.
Before interview, at least 3 days prior recommended. Bring I-901 receipt to embassy.

faqs.q: How soon can client enter USA?
faqs.a: No earlier than 30 days before I-20 program start date on valid F-1 visa.
No appeal. Reapply when circumstances materially improve—stronger ties, different program, or better funds.
Most Indian applicants attend interview. Check embassy guidance for specific renewal rules.

compliance: Client service agreement and consent must be on file before DS-160 submission
Never guarantee visa approval; avoid 'visa assured' marketing language
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, MRV, SEVIS, and third-party costs
Do not complete DS-160 with false information—client must verify accuracy
proTips: Mock interview focusing on 214(b)—why this school, why USA, who pays, what after graduation
Cross-check DS-160 against I-20 line by line before submit
Book earliest available slot after I-20 and funds are ready
For STEM OPT interest, mention as post-study plan—not immigration intent
Save complete file PDF for reapplication if refused
postApproval: Send visa foil photo and entry date rules (30-day rule) in writing
Remind client to carry I-20, I-901 receipt, and financials to port of entry
Explain SEVIS reporting and full-time enrollment obligations

timeline.title: Passport return / administrative processing if any

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Initial F-1 counselor content with 2026 MRV and SEVIS fee notes.

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: 214(b) refusals spike when client mentions settling in USA—coach return intent tied to career in India or global mobility, not permanent stay.

staffNotes.author: Quality
staffNotes.text: Mandatory mock interview for all first-time F-1 applicants.

resources.title: ICE — SEVIS I-901 fee
resources.description: Pay and print SEVIS fee receipt

resources.title: US State Dept — India visa information

quiz.question: What document proves SEVP school acceptance?
quiz.explanation: I-20 is issued by SEVP-certified school.

quiz.question: Most common refusal ground for Indian F-1 applicants?
quiz.explanation: Officers must be satisfied applicant will return after studies.

quiz.question: SEVIS I-901 fee for F-1 (2026) is approximately:
quiz.explanation: Verify current fee on ice.gov—F-1 SEVIS is USD $350.

quiz.question: Client can enter USA on F-1 no earlier than:
quiz.explanation: 30-day early entry rule applies to F-1.

dos: Verify I-20 against SEVP school list before fee collection
Pay SEVIS and complete DS-160 accurately before interview booking
Prepare client for 214(b) with concise honest answers
Show seasoned funds with sponsor ITR or loan sanction
Conduct mock interview before embassy appointment
donts: Do not coach client to lie about intent to return
Do not submit DS-160 with inconsistent travel or employment history
Do not hide prior US refusals
Do not book interview before funds and I-20 are final
mistakes: Memorised long answers sounding scripted at interview
Course unrelated to prior education without explanation
DS-160 dates not matching passport stamps

sampleDocs.title: Sample passport bio page (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Show clients which pages to scan — MRZ visible, no glare, full spread.

sampleDocs.title: Sample I-20 (mock)
sampleDocs.description: SEVP school document — SEVIS ID and program dates.

sampleDocs.title: Sample IELTS / TOEFL score report (mock)
sampleDocs.description: English proficiency for F-1 interview prep.

sampleDocs.title: Sample financial affidavit / I-134 (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Sponsor form  I-134 or bank proof for tuition + living.

sampleDocs.title: Sample bank statement (mock)
sampleDocs.description: 6-month statement with name, account number, and closing balance — explain seasoning.
